items.headline: Albemarle (ALB) Could Be 39% Undervalued On Earnings Date And Cost Saving Story
items.snippet: After a 17% slide in 30 days but a 1 year total return above 80%, Albemarle now sits at a crossroads. Fair value estimated at $212.74 vs last close $129.02. Narrative hinges on lithium prices and project execution.

items.headline: Albemarle Corporation to Release Second Quarter 2026 Earnings Results on Wednesday, August 5, 2026
items.snippet: ALB Q2 2026 earnings release scheduled for August 5, conference call August 6.

items.headline: The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
items.snippet: ALB Q1 2026 adj EBITDA +148% YoY to $664M. Company retired $1.3B debt during the quarter. Albemarle is the world largest lithium producer.

items.headline: Earnings Preview: What To Expect From Albemarle Report
items.snippet: ALB expected to release Q2 2026 earnings soon. Analysts expect EPS of $3.21 diluted, up significantly from $0.11 year-ago. FY2026 EPS projected $13.15 vs loss of $0.79 in FY2025.

items.headline: Baird Adjusts Price Target on Albemarle to $178 From $224
items.snippet: Albemarle has average analyst rating of overweight and mean price target of $211.44, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: ALB Q1 2026 sales +33% YoY. Capex cut 46%. Debt retired bringing debt-to-EBITDA to 1.0x. On track for 15% volume CAGR in Energy Storage through 2027 focusing on Greenbushes and Atacama.
